title: Couscous with chicken and chickpeas
subtitle: Moroccan spiced chicken broth poured over steamed couscous
excerpt: Chicken thighs simmered with vegetables, chickpeas, saffron, and ras el hanout into a fragrant broth, then ladled over buttered couscous. The simpler, weeknight version of the full Friday couscous — same logic, less ceremony. One pot, 45 minutes.
sourceNotes: This is a simplified domestic version of the Friday couscous — the most significant meal of the Moroccan week. The full couscous royale involves multiple proteins and traditional steaming in a couscoussier. This version uses instant couscous and a single protein, which is how it is increasingly made in Moroccan urban homes.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the broth and chicken separately from the couscous.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The broth and chicken can be made ahead. Steam the couscous fresh when serving.

content.text: The couscous absorbs the broth and becomes the flavour carrier, not a neutral starch. Use the cooking broth (not plain water) to hydrate the couscous: pour 500 ml of the hot broth over 400 g of dry couscous, cover for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork and work in butter. The remaining broth is served alongside in a bowl for people to add at the table.

The vegetables are cut large (halved carrots, whole courgette halves) so they hold their shape through the simmering time and are served alongside the chicken on the couscous, not mixed through. Moroccan couscous is presented with the meat and vegetables arranged on top of the couscous mound, with the broth poured over at the table.

Put the {{chicken-thigh}}, {{onion}}, {{ras-el-hanout}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, saffron water, and {{tinned-tomatoes}} in a large pot. Add 1.2 litres of cold water. Bring to a boil, skim the foam, then reduce to a steady simmer. Cook for 20 minutes. Add the {{carrot}} and cook for 10 minutes. Add the {{courgette}} and drained {{chickpeas}} and cook for a further 10 minutes until the vegetables are tender and the chicken is cooked through.

Put the {{couscous}} in a large bowl. Ladle 500 ml of hot broth from the pot over the couscous. Stir briefly, cover the bowl tightly, and leave for 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork. Add the {{unsalted-butter}} and work it in thoroughly with your fingers, separating every grain. Season with salt. Mound the couscous on a large serving platter. Arrange the chicken pieces, vegetables, and chickpeas on top or around the couscous. Ladle more broth over everything at the table. Serve the remaining broth and harissa separately.

items.fix: Work the butter in with your hands, lifting and rubbing the grains between your fingers for a full minute. Every grain should be separate and lightly coated

items.cause: Too much water or not enough simmering time
items.fix: The broth needs at least 40 minutes of simmering with the chicken to build flavour. If it still tastes thin, simmer uncovered for 10 minutes to reduce

items.cause: Added at the same time as courgette
items.fix: The staggered cooking order is important: add carrots 10 minutes before courgette, not at the same time

content.text: Friday couscous is the most culturally significant meal of the Moroccan week. Families gather after Friday prayers, and couscous is always at the centre of the table. This simplified version preserves the logic of the dish: spiced broth, tender chicken, vegetables, chickpeas, all served over buttered couscous with harissa on the side. It takes 45 minutes rather than 3 hours and uses instant couscous rather than the traditional steaming method, but the result is close enough to serve as a regular weeknight version of the Friday classic.
